rules.name: Profile is first-class data partition within Customer
rules.statement: A Customer may have 1..N Profiles. Almost all entities that are scoped to a Customer are also scoped to a Profile. SmsGroups.ProfileId NOT NULL and FK to Profiles is the canonical enforcement.

rules.name: A user is always on exactly 1 active profile
rules.statement: User.CurrentProfileId holds the currently active profile. Value 0 is a degenerate/uninitialised state, not a valid operating state. User switches to exactly 1 other profile at a time via explicit action.

rules.name: All capability is gated per profile, not per user
rules.statement: ProfileRoleNames (63 values) control what operations are legal for a profile. Users inherit the capabilities of their current profile, not their user roles (UserRoleName is a separate, orthogonal concept).

rules.name: Profile controls geographic send boundaries via positive lists
rules.statement: ProfilePositiveLists (per KVHX) and ProfilePosListMunicipalityCodes restrict which addresses a profile may send to. The PositiveListAddressRestriction SQL JOIN enforces this at query time.

rules.name: Profile deletion requires no active SmsGroups or Users
rules.statement: Customer.Delete() checks HasActiveProfile() before allowing deletion. Profile.Deleted=true is the soft delete state. Profiles are never hard-deleted in normal flow.

rules.name: Profile access requires explicit ProfileUserMapping
rules.statement: CanUserAccessProfile(profileId, userId) returns true only if a ProfileUserMapping row exists. Membership in a Customer does not grant access to all profiles of that customer.

rules.name: profileId==0 is a security gap — bypasses all address restrictions
rules.statement: GetAddressRestrictionForProfile(0) returns NoAddressRestriction. Multi-profile users who have not selected a profile send with no geographic restriction. This violates RULE_P004 for uninitialised sessions.

rules.name: JWT carries profileId — refresh is the synchronisation point
rules.statement: ProfileId is baked into the JWT at issuance. Profile switches update DB but not the in-flight JWT. Next token refresh reads User.CurrentProfileId from DB, propagating the switch. ServiceAlert.Contracts/WebWorkContext reads profileId from JWT claim; ServiceAlert.Api/WebWorkContext reads from DB User entity — these can diverge.

rules.name: Profile.TimeZoneId drives outbound message timing
rules.statement: NightTimeDetector.IsNightForProfile(profileId) uses Profile.TimeZoneId to determine if messages should be suppressed. Profiles in different time zones behave independently.

rules.name: ProfileApiKey scopes external integrations to a profile
rules.statement: External systems authenticate via ProfileApiKey and act with that profile's capabilities and geographic boundaries. API key type (ProfileApiKeyType) further restricts what operations are allowed.
